title: kabbalistic symbolism in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: kabbalistic symbolism in Evangelion             Quote Post by crazycashier » Mon Dec 10, 2007 3:25 pm As we all know, there are many religious references in Evangelion. The surprising majority of these stem from kabbalah and Jewish Mysticism. Any ideas, thoughts or questions concerning this aspect of NGE are welcome for post. I happen to have a background in kabbalah studies and would be more than happy to explain, debate or discuss the connections between NGE and kabbalah. All I ask is for everyone to keep and open (and clean) mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 10, 2007 4:12 pm There's a fair bit of (scattered) discussion on the topic around, that can be hunted down with the search function -- that would give the local community "state of the art" as it were. And then I'm sure that when Zugzwang spots this thread, he'll be happy to contribute.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Dec 10, 2007 4:51 pm I have some questions! What is the source of the "35 spirits" from mercy and severity as per the Tree of Emanations ( english translation here ) that we see in the opening credits of NGE? Now, my understanding is Kircher is talking about the olympic spirits of each planet (of Fear and Mercy as in his drawing), but if that's the case it means he got something mixed up along the way as "Mercy" has 40, not 35, spirits in the olympic system iirc. The rest of the diagram makes sense to me apart from that and the Biblical verses on paths 3, 5 and 7 - any ideas on what that's all about as well? I did this a while ago but didn't get around to posting it - I had a go at translating the Angel's names (and the word Evangelion, for a laugh) into hebrew so I could do some mathematical analysis of them, some of them I could look up so they are spelt correctly, but the rest... is everything spelt right then? How do I know where each name would be allotted on the tree in whichever-world? (My guess is anything ending in AL would be of Formation like the rest of the lesser angels, but as to which Number or Letter they "are", how do I work that out? What's the proper procedure?) Do you think LCL is literally the nephesch? If so, which segment of the soul would the red dots in EoE be? Unrelated to NGE - what's the significance of the minor countenance, vau and the three (not four, why?!) nails used to affix yeshuvah to the tau all sharing a common connection? I understand each component individually so you don't need to say "well, this means this and that means that" - is it really as simple as 400 + 18 i.e. V.V.V. = 18 and T. = 400? Will probably have some more questions later when I think of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Dec 10, 2007 6:47 pm Zugzwang wrote: stuff

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Dec 10, 2007 7:05 pm don't ask, plz :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Mon Dec 10, 2007 7:22 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sephiroth_%28Kabbalah%29 that's all you need.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Dec 10, 2007 8:14 pm I'm a little confused about the S-Sh spelling transition in Shamshel's and Sandalphon's names. Am I imagining things, or is Sandelphon misspelled? Sandelphon and Evangelion do not end with the same character (I mean the leftmost one, since this is hebrew) Just curious, since the numerology aspect of the Kabbalah is far beyond me.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Dec 10, 2007 11:33 pm Zugzwang wrote: incomprehensible words

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 11, 2007 2:56 am Less of the negative wet-blanket behaviour if you please A number of the shorter posts in this thread fail the "does your post contribute anything?" test.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Dec 11, 2007 8:18 am UrsusArctos wrote: I'm a little confused about the S-Sh spelling transition in Shamshel's and Sandalphon's names. Am I imagining things, or is Sandelphon misspelled? Sandelphon and Evangelion do not end with the same character (I mean the leftmost one, since this is hebrew) Just curious, since the numerology aspect of the Kabbalah is far beyond me.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Tue Dec 11, 2007 3:21 pm Does this have to do with that scene in EoE....dosen't that chart list what makes God Pwerful or something?

[]: kabbalah in NGE             Quote Post by crazycashier » Tue Dec 11, 2007 3:26 pm I don't know if my reply from last night went through, maybe I hit the delete button by mistake. In any case, I'm running late for work, and will be back tonight to re-write the reply again, I guess. I have answers about the s-sh thing as well as some of the names that zwang brought up. there is just only one time out of my day I get to go online. Be patient, and I promise I will try and answer your questions. Also, I am new at the Forum thing. If I make a mistake, let me know.

[]: kabbalistic symbolism in NGE             Quote Post by crazycashier » Wed Dec 12, 2007 4:21 am The 35 spirits as simply an anglicized version of the 35 angels of mercy and the 35 angels of severity that balance the judgement of G-d. There are equal numbers so that justice is fair, and mercy is not too forgiving. That is the religious translation. Now, the names of the Angels are tricky, because several of them are Arabic Angels. One must find their Hebrew Archangel equivilants to find a place on the tree of life for them. as far as I know, sandolphon is given the Sephirah malkhut. Since Sachiel is symbolized by Jupiter, it could be said that sachiel corresponds to Chesed. I don't know the correspondence for the rest, but I can very easily come up with one with a little research.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Dec 12, 2007 9:54 am crazycashier wrote: The 35 spirits as simply an anglicized version of the 35 angels of mercy and the 35 angels of severity that balance the judgement of G-d. There are equal numbers so that justice is fair, and mercy is not too forgiving. That is the religious translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 2:24 pm Personally, I always find Zuggy's posts very intriguing and interesting with only a few caveats: 1. This is fanwanking at its purest, finest, and most refined. Anno and co. had no clue of this stuff when they made NGE, so as long as we keep in mind it's "for fun", it's fine. 2. I usually have no clue with regards to what he's talking about. It would be helpful, Zuggy, if you'd try to summarize some points for us nincompoops. :)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:29 pm It would probably take Zugz a long time to explain all the talk of numerical analysis. Essentially, every Hebrew character has a numerical value, and adding them gives the numerical value in a certain word. The numerical value of a word or a character is very important to Kabbalists, who try to find numerical correlations between real life and scripture among other things. That's either totally incorrect or a microscopic fraction of what the Kabbalists do with regards to their numerology. I assume that the spellings of Angel names have to add up to certain values alone. Since the Arabic God is Allah and the God that made the word is Elohim (As opposed to Yahweh/Jehovah), it seems logical that an Arabic Angel too would have a name ending in al/el.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:32 pm Isn't that what Pi (the movie) was based around? Finding God in mathematical formulas?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:38 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: This is fanwanking at its purest, finest, and most refined. Anno and co. had no clue of this stuff when they made NGE, so as long as we keep in mind it's "for fun", it's fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:42 pm Jimbo had his brain frozen in the 60 hours he was without electricity. He is now hooked up to the SkyNet computer and is typing with a personality modification chip. How do you like my typing? Call 1-800-555-5555

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:43 pm BrikHaus wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: This is fanwanking at its purest, finest, and most refined. Anno and co. had no clue of this stuff when they made NGE, so as long as we keep in mind it's "for fun", it's fine.
